threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus to get to the station instead of just walking five minutes to the tube. Proper locals don’t bother with the bus unless it’s raining.
Caught the N1 last week; a couple argued about who lost the last kebab, while the driver sighed like he’d seen it all before. Classic late night vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy wearing lederhosen at the bakery, casually picking up a croissant like it was the most normal thing.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can a kebab shop serve vegan döner at 3 AM while the street DJ mixes techno with '90s pop hits. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate around the BART delays; they know to avoid Civic Center without breaking a sweat—or even look at a schedule.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo arguing with a pigeon at the Civic Center station. Must’ve been a tough night for someone.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think we spend siesta time napping, but the real locals are out gardening or fixing up the front of their pisos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the unpredictable train schedules. Only locals seem unfazed by the chaos.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch at the sound of the bus brakes outside your window. Locals have mastered the art of sleeping through it.
Heard a bloke on the N37 loudly arguing with a pigeon about whether it flew or walked. The pigeon looked indifferent, classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I spotted a guy playing bagpipes in front of a kebab shop while people casually walked by, like it was an everyday symphony.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings. It's like the city's pulse beats different here, unpredictable.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their reaction to a BART delay; it’s just another Tuesday, while newcomers are ready to freak out.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling three apples while waiting for the 31. The bus rolled by, and he just kept juggling—never breaking focus. Classic Tenderloin moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella for lunch. Most of us go for a 'bocadillo' and a 'cervesa' at the bar instead. Much simpler.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by the way they insist on ordering paella for lunch, completely unaware it’s a dinner dish here.
